title: 🐛 Fix JSON Schema "not" keyword
body:
The JSON Schema "not" keyword expects a JSON Schema object instead of a `List`.

As per most recent 2019-09: https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-02#section-9.2.1.4

(and older draft 4: https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-fge-json-schema-validation-00#section-5.5.6)

title: Relax hard-pinning of starlette
body:
[`starlette`](https://github.com/encode/starlette/releases) is up to `0.13.4` now.

Unless a specific patch release is actually a hard requirement it's more user-friendly to pin to a [compatible release](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#compatible-release) so as to *automatically* benefit from bug/security fixes.
comments:
# [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=h1) Report
> Merging [#1555](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=desc) into [master](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/commit/072c2bc7f9d4900c8afe75f2775942a63325c569&el=desc) will **not change** coverage.
> The diff coverage is `n/a`.

[![Impacted file tree graph](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555/graphs/tree.svg?width=650&height=150&src=pr&token=llDQVnTfnB)](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=tree)

```diff
@@            Coverage Diff            @@
##            master     #1555   +/-   ##
=========================================
  Coverage   100.00%   100.00%           
=========================================
  Files          230       230           
  Lines         6862      6862           
=========================================
  Hits          6862      6862           
```



------

[Continue to review full report at Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=continue).
> **Legend** - [Click here to learn more](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/codecov-delta)
> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=footer). Last update [072c2bc...d1459de](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555?src=pr&el=lastupdated). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments).

Thanks! Yep, the problem is that as FastAPI is very tightly integrated with Starlette, there could be (and have been) breaking changes very easily, even for patch releases :pensive: 

For Pydantic it's possible to have more relaxed pinning mainly because Pydantic runs all of FastAPI's tests as part of its own CI tests.

On the other hand, I'm thinking of migrating from Flit to Poetry, that way I could set up Dependabot to make it send PRs for each new version.

But for the time being, I have to keep the strict pinning...

Anyway, thanks for your contribution! And for all the management in Conda Forge! :bow: :rocket:

title: Generated new translation directory to support Italian docs
body:
Set up the docs to support Italian by following the instructions [here](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/contributing/#new-language) and using the following command:

`python ./scripts/docs.py new-lang it`

A new `index.md` was generated under `it/docs` which we'll need to translate as a next step. This will help us get started with our efforts in #1556.

title: ✨ Deep merge responses
body:
Deep merge responses to ensure that all the metadata added in `responses` parameters in *path operations* is kept and properly merged with the generated default response, keeping all the data from both and using proper default/overwrites.

This includes https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1563 from @rkbeatss :rocket: :sparkles: 

It actually started as an update to https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1563 , but I realized the problem was a lot deeper than I was initially thinking, so it ended up in a big refactor. :sweat_smile:  

And to avoid conflicts, I rebased it all from `master`, after all the recent merges. :twisted_rightwards_arrows:

review_comments:
```suggestion
Questo è solo un **breve tutorial / ripasso** sui type hints di Python. Copre solamente il minimo necessario per imparare a usarli con **FastAPI**... Quindi molto poco.
```

Should we use Italic for foreign words?
```suggestion
**FastAPI** è interamente basato sui *type hints* per i numerosi vantaggi che essi offrono.
```

Not sure if can be done:
```suggestion
* Li <abbr title="Li unisce in una singola entità. Inserisce prima i contenuti della prima, poi dell'altra. In inglese: *concatenate*">concatena</abbr> inserendo uno spazio in mezzo.
```

It seems that something is missing, but I'm not sure. It's been a while since I last spoke Italian. Does my suggestion make sense?
```suggestion
Osserva questa funzione, puoi vedere che ha già le annotazioni di tipo:
```

```suggestion
Puoi dichiarare tutti i tipi *standard* di Python, non solo `str`.
```
Grazie, e complimenti per il tuo italiano 🙂

```suggestion
Attenzione che non è uguale a dichiarare i valori di default dei parametri della funzione:
```

```suggestion
Adesso immagina che stai ancora creando quella funzione, ma ora utilizzando i type hints.
```

title: OpenAPI docs for scopes defined on a single route
body:
Scopes that are defined on an individual route as part of a `Security` Dependency were not being returned on the OpenAPI JSON that is generated.

This seems to be because when the dependencies are flattened the `security_scopes` are not being flattened (unlike `security_requirements` and others which are). This sets the default scopes to an empty list and flattens the scopes of sub dependencies. I've added a test which confirms this in the OAuth2 test.

I'm brand new to the FastAPI code base so I am unsure if there are any unintended consequences with this approach - but all the tests seem to pass and the new behaviour makes sense in my understanding of what the code should do.

> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1596?src=pr&el=footer). Last update [b87072b...5544689](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1596?src=pr&el=lastupdated). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments).

Awesome, thanks @rkbeatss ! :rocket: :cake: 

I updated it a bit and included an optional parameter `root_path_in_servers` in case someone doesn't want the auto-generated servers. And I added some docs for that.

Given this solves the generation of OpenAPI schemas and the utils no longer need the `openapi_prefix`, I removed that parameter that was recently added, so, back to its original form :man_facepalming: 

I also checked/confirmed that this doesn't break the functionality with OAuth2 tokens by the spec, and to make sure they all stay compatible, I made those `tokenUrl`s relative URLs, that way those docs will keep working as-is even when behind a reverse proxy.

Thanks for your contribution! :coffee: :rocket: :tada:

review_comments:
```suggestion
...pode demorar, dependendo do meu tempo disponível e outros fatores. 😅 🎉
```

```suggestion
Você pode usar um gerador de projetos para começar, por já incluir configurações iniciais, segurança, banco de dados e os primeiros _endpoints_ API já feitos para você.
```

```suggestion
Um gerador de projetos sempre terá uma pré-configuração que você pode atualizar e adaptar para suas próprias necessidades, mas pode ser um bom ponto de partida para seu projeto.
```

```suggestion
    * **Rápido**: Alta performance, no nível de **NodeJS** e **Go** (graças ao Starlette e Pydantic).
```

Suporte não eh mensurável. 
```suggestion
    * **Intuitivo**: Ótimo suporte de editor. <abbr title="também conhecido como auto-complete, auto completação, IntelliSense">_Auto-Complete_</abbr> em todo lugar. Menos tempo _debugando_.
```

```suggestion
* Modelos básicos para usuários (modifique e remova conforme suas necessidades).
```
Será que seria interessante traduzir warning pra atenção? Ou já é um padrão?
Faltou um espaço entre o . e o Com

```suggestion
    * **Robusto**: Tenha código pronto para produção. Com documentação interativa automática.
```

Good point!

I'm not sure, I would imagine yes, but as there are some words that work better in English (even in Portuguese), I'll leave it like this for now. And we can update it later if there's a consensus on that in the Portuguese Translations issue: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1211

But meanwhile, it should be fine and we can have this merged :rocket:

@marco-neumann-by just to point out that the issue with httpx that we hung up on is resolved now

Awesome! Great job @empicano ! Thanks for your contribution! :clap: :memo: :nerd_face: :cake: 

Thanks everyone for the discussion (and @florimondmanca for HTTPX :rocket: :blue_heart: ).
review_comments: You probably need to add `trust_env=False` here as well, otherwise the async client might go through a proxy. Not sure if this is an upstream bug though.

My initial thought was to simply follow the [HTTPX example](https://www.python-httpx.org/async/#calling-into-python-web-apps) for calling into python web apps. Also, HTTPX sets [some constraints on the proxies it accepts](https://github.com/encode/httpx/blob/cfd07cc290cc610fceb8d9f025c6bebc1d4b3275/httpx/_utils.py#L287) from the environment, I don't know if that changes anything.

Otherwise:
```suggestion
    async with AsyncClient(app=main.app, base_url='http://test', trust_env=False) as ac:
```
https://github.com/encode/httpx/issues/1039 I think it might be a bug.
Hm, interesting. What do you propose, we add the `trust_env=False` for now?
Yes, I think that's reasonable. Users who have some default network proxy active (e.g. for privacy reasons or in company network situations) will get some bizarre test failures.

Hi there, chiming in to confirm that this odd behavior is indeed due to a bug in HTTPX.

So it might be okay to go with this as-is (i.e. without the `trust_env=False` hack), keeping in mind that https://github.com/encode/httpx/issues/1039 looks to be fairly straight-forward to fix (in case anyone wants to give it a go - happy to review a PR, but no expectations of course), so hopefully a fix will be released soon and users equipped with proxies won't encounter this.

title: Raise form early
body:
Addresses #1599.

We'll be leveraging the `[import].__package__` field as a way to determine if the CORRECT multipart import was used or not.

Oddly enough, the incorrect `multipart` import will have a `.__package__` field value of "" (empty string). The correct import, `python-multipart`, will have a `.__package__` field value of "multipart".

We'll check to see if the user will be using forms or not with `is_form_data(BodyFieldInfo)`. If this evaluates to true, then we can check to see there is a `multipart` import at all by using `importlib.util.find_spec("multipart")`. If that again evaluates to true, we can check the `.__package__` value to see if it is an empty string or not.

Alternative solution:

Instead of testing the imports' `.__package__` field, we could try to wrap a try / catch with a method exclusive to `python-multipart` that is not in `multipart` instead. If an exception was caught we know it's also the wrong import.

`parser = multipart.QuerystringParser()` might be a good method to try out.

If we're okay with this solution, we can clean it up a little bit + throw those Exceptions.
I saw there's a logging module already in fastapi, would that be the correct one to use?

Not too sure if this is the best way to detect it. This test will fail if the user doesn't have any kind of `multipart` installed. Maybe we skip it if we can't import `multipart`? thoughts? 

Alternatively, we add our own mock `multipart` package and change the sys path so that it gets imported when the code is run?
Also, I'd like to maybe add more specific assertions for the error message possibly as they both raise `RuntimeError`

If you print `error` you'll see that there are a lot of spaces on it.
```suggestion
            error = (
                "Form data requires [python-multipart] to be installed. Currently "
                "[multipart] is installed and not compatible with [python-multipart]. "
                "Uninstall [multipart] and then install [python-multipart]."
            )
```

What do you think about this?
```suggestion
    return field in (params.Form, params.File, bytes)
```
I didn't know you could do this to get rid of the spaces; thanks for this suggestion 😄
I don't know how to create a multi-line suggestion, does anyone know?
Looks good to me, will commit this suggestion. 👍

And the updated docs about `root_path`, that previously required the `openapi_prefix` parameter, are here: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/behind-a-proxy/

This is available in FastAPI version `0.59.0` :tada: 

So I'll close this now, but thanks for your effort! :coffee:

title: ✅ Update response_model_by_alias
body:
Update logic for `response_model_by_alias` to apply it only to the `response_model`, not to the object returned by the function.

And update tests with a small note in the tests clarifying (so I don't forget) that `response_model_by_alias=False` is basically just a quick hack that doesn't support OpenAPI.

The proper way to do it if the intention is to have OpenAPI support is to create another model (possibly inheriting from the original one).

This supersedes https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1412

title: Simplify usage of RedirectResponse/FileResponse
body:
A simple solution to #1628:

```python3
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.responses import RedirectResponse, PlainTextResponse

app = FastAPI()

@app.get('/', response_class=PlainTextResponse)
async def hello():
    return 'Hello world!'

@app.get('/redir', response_class=RedirectResponse, status_code=303)
async def redir():
    return '/'
```

```
$ curl -iL "http://127.0.0.1:8000/redir" -H "accept: */*"
HTTP/1.1 303 See Other
date: Sun, 28 Jun 2020 12:44:30 GMT
server: uvicorn
location: /
transfer-encoding: chunked

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
date: Sun, 28 Jun 2020 12:44:30 GMT
server: uvicorn
content-length: 12
content-type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

Hello world!
```
comments: This PR also adds a wrapper to `FileResponse`, so with defined `response_class=FileResponse` one can return a path string from endpoint function.
The next step would be to change `status_code: int = 200` in `applications.py` to `status_code: int = None` and allow response classes to set their default codes if other was not specified.

Thanks for the effort @dmig! :coffee: :rocket: 

I implemented this in a different way here: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3457, without modifying the underlying response classes. And I also handled the default `status_code`, extracting it from the response classes (that part was tricky :sweat_smile: ).

It will be available tomorrow with FastAPI version `0.66.0` :tada: :rocket: 

Given that, I'll close this PR now. But thanks for your effort!

title: Exclude all routes from schema under an APIRouter
body:
Proof of Concept for issue #1650.

You can pass `include_in_schema=False` to `APIRouter` to exclude all routes in schema from this router.

review_comments: 各セクションは前のセクションを踏まえた内容になっています。しかし、トピックごとに分割されているので、特定のAPIの要求を満たすようなトピックに直接たどり着けるようになっています。
また、エディターで使用することで、書く必要のあるコードがどれだけ少ないか、すべての型チェック、自動補完などを確認して、FastAPIの利点を実感することができます。
備考
以下は、アプリケーションを本番環境にデプロイしたいと思ったときに、おそらく行うであろうことです:
また、サーバーとして動作するように`uvicorn`をインストールします。

御存知かもしれませんが、マークダウンの「!!! note」の記法は少なくとも4種類あって、
以下の記号と英単語のペアになって表示されます。(記号は近いものを使っています) 日本語の記事だと全て備考かなと思っています。
区別するために、自分が翻訳しているものでは右の日本語を充てているんですが、どれもしっくりきていないのでまとめて意見を頂けるとありがたいです。（きれいに区別できていないので意訳できていないです。辞書を引いて最初にでてきた単語を使っています）

* ✏️ note: 注意
* ℹ️ info: 情報
* 🔥 tip: 豆知識
* ✔️  check: 確認

丁寧にありがとうございます。
noteについては「備考」だと思います。
「注意」だとwarningと捉えられそうです。
その他3つの日本語については異論ありません。

ありがとうございます。では、この4つは「備考、情報、豆知識、確認」に統一しましょうか。
ただ、原文で「!!! note "hogehoge"」の様に指定されている場合はそちらの翻訳を優先させた方がいいと思います。

> ただ、原文で「!!! note "hogehoge"」の様に指定されている場合はそちらの翻訳を優先させた方がいいと思います。

これについては私も同意見です。
このチュートリアルは**FastAPI**のほぼすべての機能の使い方をステップバイステップで紹介します。
また、エディターで使用することで、FastAPIの利点が実感できます。作成する必要があるコードの量、すべての型チェック、自動補完などを確認できます。
これは、アプリケーションを本番環境にデプロイしたいと思ったときに、おそらく実施するであろうことです。
サーバーとして稼働させる場合は`uvicorn`もインストールしてください:
また、利用したいオプションの依存関係のそれぞれについても同じです。

title: Fix app.extra annotation
body:
`**kwargs` annotation is based on the value.
ATM extra is annotated like `Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]` which causes issues with type-checkers.

All of them.
Here's an example:

I doubt the build fails because of the PR.

For reference, it's described here: https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/getting_started.html?highlight=kwargs#more-function-signatures

Thanks for your contribution! :coffee: :cake:

title: Allow None as a valid JSON value
body:
ATM, if you receive a post with the following json: `{data: null}`

And you have the following definition:
```
@app.post("/")
def post_data(data: Optional[str]):
  pass
```

FastAPI will say `data` was not passed. That's because it internally uses `None` as a placeholder for non-existing fields, instead of a sentinel value.

title: Update isort script to match changes in the new release, isort v5.0.2
body:
Apparently, isort latest release ```isort v5.0.2``` is missing the ```--recursive or -rc``` flag and since the project uses the flag in it's script, it causes the tests to fail. An alternative to this is a dot, as in ```isort .```.

#### Missing flags
```
--apply
--recursive
```
#### Replacement
```
isort .
```

For more information on these changes see:
https://github.com/timothycrosley/isort/blob/develop/CHANGELOG.md#500-penny---july-4-2020

With this change, the build should be okay.

重複している。
Pythonのフォーマット文字列と同様の構文で、パスの「パラメータ」や「変数」を宣言できます:
標準のPythonの型アノテーションを使用して、関数内のパスパラメータの型を宣言できます:
!!! check 確認
したがって、型宣言を使用すると、**FastAPI**は自動でリクエストの <abbr title="HTTPリクエストで受け取った文字列をPythonデータへ変換する">"解析"</abbr> を行います。
すべてのデータバリデーションは Pydantic によって内部で実行されるため、Pydanticの全てのメリットが得られます。そして、安心して利用することができます。

title: Update isort command to use the new releases of isort v5.0.2
body:
Apparently, isort latest release ```isort v5.0.2``` is missing the ```--recursive or -rc``` flag and since the project uses the flag in it's script, it causes the tests to fail. An alternative to this is a dot, as in ```isort .```.

#### Missing flags
```
--apply
--recursive
```
#### Replacement
```
isort .
```

For more information on these changes see:
https://github.com/timothycrosley/isort/blob/develop/CHANGELOG.md#500-penny---july-4-2020

With this change, the build should be okay.
comments: Why have you closed this issue? Is it a bad idea to upgrade isort?

review_comments: 同じサポートは受けられません

Noteが追加されています

!!! note "備考"
FastAPIはデフォルト値が`=None`なので、`q`の値が必要ないことがわかります。
`Optional[str]`の`Optional`はFastAPIでは使用されませんが、エディタがより良いサポートを提供し、エラーを検出できるようになります。
この項目は削除されたようです。

en版ではinfoの部分がガラッと変わっているようです。

データを送るには、`POST` (もっともよく使われる)、`PUT`、`DELETE` または `PATCH` を使わなければいけません。
`GET` リクエストでボディを送信することは、仕様では未定義の動作ですが、FastAPI でサポートされており、非常に複雑な（極端な）ユースケースにのみ対応しています。
奨励されていないので、Swagger UIを使った対話型のドキュメントでは`GET`を使ったときにボディのドキュメントが表示されず、途中のプロキシが対応していない可能性があります。

title: feat: return OpenAPI content type
body:
According to https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/issues/110, the OAI TSC approved the use of an official mime/content type for OpenAPI documents in 2018:

- `application/vnd.oai.openapi`
- `application/vnd.oai.openapi+json`

This PR updates FastAPI to set the right content type header when returning the OpenAPI at `/openapi.json`. It adds a small test to ensure it gets set properly.

Setting this content type helps tools which may try to access your API description understand what type of description it is, in this case OpenAPI. It's especially useful when the URI is *not* `/openapi.json` since it could be anything by setting the FastAPI `openapi_url` setting.

こちらも消し忘れ
ドキュメントのタイプミスを修正
```suggestion 既存のissuesを確認して、他の人を助けてみてください。皆さんが回答を知っているかもしれない質問がほとんどです。🤓 ``` 微妙ですが、「見」は変換しない方が意味があってる気がします。 🤔 「ような」はない方が読みやすいかもしれません。

```suggestion
GitHub issuesでは良い回答を得やすい質問ができるように、もしくは、質問する前に自身で解決できるようにテンプレートがガイドしてくれます。そして、GitHubではたとえ時間がかかっても全てに答えているか確認できます。個人的にはGitterチャットでは同じことはできないです。😅
```

語順の入れかえ

### 
```suggestion
以下のスポンサーになることもできます:
```

タイポです。

title: WIP: Populate summary and description of an endpoint from its docstring
body:
**Note!** Please see https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1633 for context why I think this is a good idea.

**Note!** This is still work in progress and missing, among other things:

- Documentation
- Unit testing the new changes
- I'll need to make the way to "parse" the summary and description lines from the docstring cover all cases, like badly formatted docstrings and different line ending styles.

I'm sharing this PR to gather feedback if the maintainers agree it is a good idea and I should proceed with cleaning up this patch.

--

This commit changes how the OpenAPI summary and description for an endpoint are populated from the docstring of the function implementing the endpoint.

Old behavior: Only the description is populated from the docstring

New behavior: Both the summary and the description are populated from
the docstring unless explicitly provided as decorator arguments

The new behavior is more aligned with PEP 257 specifying that a docstring consists of summary line and an extended description, neatly mapping to the corresponding fields in OpenAPI specification.
comments:
Thanks for your interest!

Hmm, I see that it wouldn't be easy to *not* use that new behavior, and it would require everyone to adopt that convention that might not work very well for all the applications.

I also prefer to have the independent `summary` parameter that can be found with autocompletion.

That on top of being a breaking change that would be incompatible with existing applications. And that the summary is currently already auto-generated from the function name when not provided.

So I'll pass on this one, but thanks for your interest! :coffee:

title: Compatible with pydantic exporting model's method
comments:
@xl9211 Could you explain what you're doing exactly?

But anyhow, I've noticed that `jsonable_encoders` has a `set()` as default parameter, we should remove it:
- https://docs.quantifiedcode.com/python-anti-patterns/correctness/mutable_default_value_as_argument.html?highlight=mutable

Code snippet examplifying problem:
```
def func(a: int, param = set()):
    param.add(a)
    print(param)

func(1) # prints "{1}"
func(2) # prints "{1, 2}"
```

Thanks for your interest, it seems you are assuming Pydantic 1.x. I can't remove the backward-compatibility code until I deprecate Pydantic 0.32.0.

I should do that soon, but this can't be done before that.

So I'll pass on this for now. Thanks for your effort! :cake: :coffee:

I should do that soon, but this can't be done before that.

So I'll pass on this for now. Thanks for your effort! :cake: :coffee:
review_comments:
Why did you remove these line?
Considering the type of variables, i think there is no possibility of removing these lines.
When you set "None" as default,  you will need to check condition of existence in the rest of the code.

When exclude is dict, that thing were wrong.
I user pydantic's advanced case.

Also see:
https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/usage/exporting_models/#advanced-include-and-exclude

title: Remove mutable types from default arguments
body:
As noticed by @Kludex [here](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1720#issuecomment-660274476) there set and dict in defaults. Current logic for `exclude` already covers case with None, and `custom_encoder` got checks `if custom_encoder:` which cover None case but I can add additional check for dict which is unnecessary imo
Also I added Optional TypeHint for keyword arguments with None as default

3.6 build failing because of last pydantic update. I can dig a bit more to check how to mitigate this later
Found several more cases with mutables as default values, will make separate PR for it later

Thanks for your interest!

There's actually no need for that, those container types should only be dangerous if they are mutated by the functions. But they are never mutated, their content is checked or they are copied, so it's fine to leave it as it is.

But thanks for your effort! :coffee: :cake:

@tiangolo 
Then there is unnecessary line (check for None) https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/52f0f8657e93c5ad72568e7b71dafb8c4fc9f9d6/fastapi/encoders.py#L48
since according to annotation `exclude` not Optional and can't be None
And another moment (more beauty then functionality) - include and exclude have common behavior but one have None as default and second - set.
Both makes no difference in functionality and great as it is, just a matter of taste
review_comments:
I think this line needs change. When "exclude" is None, "not exclude" is true and "key not in exclude" does not check. 
```suggestion
                and ((include and key in include) or (exclude and key not in exclude))
```

you can check my previous commit: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1730/commits/3be96857187d1e72f21777bf709455c503ead87d I did exactly the same, but it won't work:
There we check do we need this value or not, you suggestion: `If include exists and key in include we need it, or if exclude exists and key not in exists `
But what about case when exclude not exists or empty? In this case we return False and doesn't return value
My logic:
`If include exists and key in include we need it, or if exclude not exists or key not in exists` - It case when exclude not exists or empty.

Your logic was excellent.
Now I realized why my change doesn't work.
Thank you for your patch and sorry for my mistakes.
No problem :) As I said, I made this mistake too

title: Simplify code syntax in several places
body:
Hi guys,
I enjoy to work with `FastAPI`, it's really great 👍 

What was done in scope of this PR:
* Use comprehension instead of loops.
* Use `__init__` method of a base class where it possible.
* Replace usage of `dict.setdefault ` method with `collections.defaultdict`.

في أربعاء، 22 يوليو، 2020 في 12:05 ص، كتب Angelo Cutaia <
notifications@github.com>:

> *@acutaia* approved this pull request.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1753#pullrequestreview-452788702>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AQIQIRI6FDMMKZR25EWXMZLR4XYILANCNFSM4PDNA2UA>
> pjjjiii.
>
Thanks for your effort @uriyyo ! :rocket: 

And thanks for the discussion everyone. :coffee: 

Nice trick with the `defaultdict`! :nerd_face: 

I reverted a couple of the changes, some lists, because I find it more explicit with an explicit list than with a list comprehension when the logic starts to have some extra complexity.

For some of the classes, I had intentionally not used `super()` at some point after some breaking changes in Starlette, and because there wasn't that much shared code, I had decided to duplicate the small bits, given I still had to implement the custom local logic.

And for the security classes, they don't really have any internal logic, they only set attributes, so I found it more explicit to duplicate those little bits that still needed some customization for each specific class in several cases than to pass everything to `super()`, as it seems to hide some virtual complexity that doesn't really exist. The amount of code is quite similar, and it's still needed to override some things (like the Pydantic models). So I prefer to keep it that way.

Thanks for your contribution! :rocket: :cake:
review_comments:
Is there a reason for removing the current logic?
```suggestion
            path=path, endpoint=endpoint, name=get_name(endpoint) if name is None else name,
```
(the suggestion will probably make black unhappy)

Case, when `name` argument is None, has already covered at the base class and it used the same `get_name ` function.

This is a code of a part of a `starllet.routing.WebSocketRoute` class:
```python
class WebSocketRoute(BaseRoute):
    def __init__(
        self, path: str, endpoint: typing.Callable, *, name: str = None
    ) -> None:
        assert path.startswith("/"), "Routed paths must start with '/'"
        self.path = path
        self.endpoint = endpoint
        self.name = get_name(endpoint) if name is None else name

        if inspect.isfunction(endpoint) or inspect.ismethod(endpoint):
            # Endpoint is function or method. Treat it as `func(websocket)`.
            self.app = websocket_session(endpoint)
        else:
            # Endpoint is a class. Treat it as ASGI.
            self.app = endpoint

        self.path_regex, self.path_format, self.param_convertors = compile_path(path)
```

There we can get overhead since both `WebSocketRoute` and `APIWebSocketRoute` will call `websocket_session`.
Currently it only makes coroutine, so nothing bad happens

Same here. Not quite big issue, but we assigning `HTTPBaseModel` to `self.model` and right after super() we assign `HTTPBearerModel`. In current flow it won't make any difference, but can lead to mistakes in future

I totally agree with you.

I think we should respect base classes and invoke their `__init__` methods, that why I did so.
In case if it wrong I will revert to the base version. 

@SirTelemak what is your opinion?

Agree, with you. 
But it will be called only at the start-up time so it should be a big performance issue.
And we remove code duplication (init logic from base class).

@SirTelemak what is your opinion?

I agree with you about removing duplication and that's great catch and it not making performance issues at all currently since it just return coroutine (not the result). BUT! Starlette is external module and logic can be changed in any moment and that's makes me nervous a bit :) @Kludex what do you think about it?
Unlike previous point, this one is okay with `super()` just wanted to draw you attention here

I think the best option here is to extract the same logic of `HTTPBearer` and `HTTPBase` to base class.
Looks like `HTTPBearer` doesn't match to the logic of a base `HTTPBase` class.

> Starlette is external module and logic can be changed in any moment and that's makes me nervous a bit :) 

We set the package versions, so I don't see that as an issue. If Starlette changes, we need to adapt to keep following the latest Starlette :stuck_out_tongue:

Hi @MashhadiNima, 

I update this function in my PR #1753.
I used `collections.defaultdict` in order to replace usage of `dict.setdafault`.

Hi dear @uriyyo
Thank you for informing me of this.
My goal was to eliminate the second `for` loop. In the next commit, I will use `collections.defaultdict` to make the code cleaner.

> My goal was to eliminate the second `for` loop. In the next commit, I will use `collections.defaultdict` to make the code cleaner.

@MashhadiNima  What is the reason to eliminate the second `for` loop?
There can be a performance issue if we will use `tuple` concatenation in every `for` iteration.
```python
encoders_by_class_tuples[encoder] += (type_,)  # this line will create new tuple every time
```

Correct me if I missed smth.

I eliminate the second `for` loop for better performance.
`encoders_by_class_tuples[encoder] += (type_,)` create new tuple in every time but it's faster than cascades each list to tuple.
I tested it again and found cascading got bad performance.
@MashhadiNima My bad, just tested it out, you are totally right.

> Why?

Because the class ‍‍‍"FastAPIError" inherits "RuntimeError" and doesn't have anything additional.
In my opinion, it would be better to use "RuntimeError" directly.

> > Why?
> 
> Because the class ‍‍‍"FastAPIError" inherits "RuntimeError" and doesn't have anything additional.
> In my opinion, it would be better to use "RuntimeError" directly.

FastAPIError should'n add anything additional, it just helps you to find the source of mistake. RuntimeError is built-in and can be evoked by several other libraries and it will be hard to find exception source. But `FastAPIError` is specific for FastAPI. 
For example, you now your code can raise FastAPIError and what to mitigate this, you do something like this
```python
try:
    func_that_may_rise_error()
except FastAPIError:
    log.info('it\'s okay, we can handle this')
    handle_error()
except RuntimeError:
    log.exception('something weird happened, take a look')
    raise
```

Thanks for your explanation. 
My idea isn't good. sorry to all. I will close this PR.

Thanks for the help here @SirTelemak ! :cake: 

And thanks @MashhadiNima for the interest. :nerd_face:
review_comments: Why should we stop with the `RuntimeError`? Better use `raise Exception`, for `RuntimeError` is a descendant of an `Exception` class.
`Exception` is general. Using `RuntimeError` when the exception is `RuntimeError` makes the code more intelligible.
So does `FastAPIError`

title: ✨ Add `description` parameter to all the security scheme classes, e.g. `APIKeyQuery(name="key", description="A very cool API key")`
body:
My suggestion is to add a description parameter to the `__init__` methods of `APIKeyQuery(SecurityBase):`, `APIKeyHeader(SecurityBase):` and `APIKeyCookie(SecurityBase):` and use it to initialize the `APIKey` object. This allows for a description in the `securitySchemes` objects of the `openapi.json` and therefore in the Swagger and Redoc documentation.

`class SecurityBase(BaseModel):` already has an attribute `description: Optional[str] = None`.
`class APIKey(SecurityBase):` inherits from SecurityBase.

The classes `APIKeyQuery(SecurityBase):`, `APIKeyHeader(SecurityBase):` and `APIKeyCookie(SecurityBase):` initialize an `APIKey` object in their `__init__` method. They initialize it with some kwargs and the `name` parameter, but currently without a description parameter.

Example: using `APIKeyQuery(name="X-Auth-Key", auto_error=False, description="My description")` would look likt this in Redoc:
![image w:400](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20515386/88054300-64b1ea80-cb5d-11ea-915a-20e6f8e071fa.png)

title: Updates starlette dependency to 0.13.6 due to vulnerability in 0.13.4
body:
The project has Starlett dependency of version 0.13.4 which has a directory traversal vulnerability for windows machines. I was made aware of this through: https://snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-STARLETTE-573266

This upgrade would mostly be for the fix to this vulnerability that was introduced in Starlette 0.13.5. This upgrade would be to version 0.13.6 because version 0.13.6 fixes a breaking change related to how 0.13.5 was initially implemented.

Here is a link to the Starlett issue that was fixed for more details: https://github.com/encode/starlette/issues/981

title: 💄 Use OS preference theme
body:
Related to #725 

MkDocs [implemented a feature](https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material/getting-started/#color-scheme) to set the theme based on the user's OS preference.

This is what the website will look like if the user's preferred theme is dark:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88103492-64d3d980-cba1-11ea-8031-d5d5924720cd.png)

For the light theme users:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88103632-8cc33d00-cba1-11ea-9208-ae7d464ada17.png)

I placed my cursor on `Languages` so that you can see that the accent is still there.

📝 Docs preview for commit 91bd93800815f1e09ffcddf4a0f060d3b7691e73 at: https://5f18d8c5bb3cc340e3f2c379--fastapi.netlify.app
📝 Docs preview for commit 49e921e09087b1cfd03d451aa91d139044cddfee at: https://5f30826f7ce85cd72596308a--fastapi.netlify.app

In fact we keep '$ref' (instead of 'allOf' + '$ref' ) for enums without custom title, description or default value.
So no need to change anything on _fastapi_ side

title: add: pass swagger js, url and docs favicon
body:
Hi,
there is some changes to be able to fix swagger-ui's url on FastApi class creation
That might help to avoid issues like https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/6254 and hacks like dadd6650ed0418722ff3e39364fed53460ec62ea in the future from the developers perspective
This might also be helpful if someone don`t want to use jsdelivr's cdn

[Continue to review full report at Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1771?src=pr&el=continue).
> **Legend** - [Click here to learn more](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/codecov-delta)
> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1771?src=pr&el=footer). Last update [a63b1ef...26fa931](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1771?src=pr&el=lastupdated). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments).

I don't really like how I hardcoded url's in both `fastapi/applications.py` and `fastapi/openapi/docs.py`, but `get_swagger_ui_html` is also used in `docs_src/extending_openapi/tutorial002.py` and `tests/test_local_docs.py` and changing these files could be an even worse idea.
📝 Docs preview for commit 26fa931fce5ba17cd19bf7044574f1e5a14fb3c6 at: https://5f18812e074b3d9af463dc25--fastapi.netlify.app

Thanks for your interest!

Currently, it's fairly easy to add the docs UI manually with any level of customization wanted as described here: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/extending-openapi/#include-the-custom-docs

And the number of possible parameters and configs to pass to `FastAPI()` could be enormous if I tried to support all the possible configurations, and I prefer to recommend customizing the docs UI directly.

So I'm gonna pass on this one. But thanks for your effort! :cake: :coffee:

title: Added flake8
body:
I added `flake8` plugin for validating Python code style.
`flake8` added to dependencies list, `format` script to format and clean all your code, and `test` script.
I ran `flake8` on all files in fastapi and tests and changed them as well.

title: ⬆️ Bump mkdocs-material
body:
Following #1760, highlighted code color in the dark theme was nearly impossible to read, as @AngliD stated in [#725](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/725#issuecomment-662447763).

Later in that issue, @squidfunk stated that by bumping `mkdocs-material` to `5.5.0`, the colors will be better.

- Light theme before:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88455944-fd0be000-ce79-11ea-8c2e-ca769571a5eb.png)

- Light theme now:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88455955-1ca30880-ce7a-11ea-9ae7-1ab1e7510b06.png)

- Dark theme before:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88455966-393f4080-ce7a-11ea-8933-3c7cc9207890.png)

- Dark theme now:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19605940/88455973-48be8980-ce7a-11ea-8af9-90eb978d712b.png)

review_comments: завдяки Starlette та Pydantic
Збільшує швидкість розробки на 200 - 300 відсотків.
Зменшує можливість виникнення людських помилок на 40 відсотків.
Хороша робота з редактором.
Якщо ви розробляєте? (not sure)
задумливого шару?
API це воно чи він?

редактор це все ж програма. Може краще:
>Тут вказано як налаштувати підказки у Вашому IDE

*конфігурацією
*потрібно
Конструкція речення нетипова для української мови, коли речення починається з "Це ...". Незрозуміло до чого відноситься "це"
```suggestion Також Вам потрібен ASGI сервер для production етапу, наприклад Uvicorn або Hypercorn. ```

```suggestion
* `--reload`: перезавантажує сервер після кожної зміни в коді. Використовується лише при розробці.
```
```suggestion ```
Версія is better because of singular 'verion' in English ```suggestion ```

```suggestion
**Typer** це молодший брат FastAPI. Він призначенний бути **FastAPI для CLIs**. ⌨️ 🚀
```

```suggestion
Якщо Ваш код містить `async` / `await`, використовуйте `async def`:
```

```suggestion
Запустіть сервер за допомого команди:
```

I absolutely agree about the upper case. It more looks like a posh language with a reference to a "big cheese" though you can refer to one person (singular) with "Ви" it is completely wrong if you mean the plural.
Можна просто "тестуйте API напряму"
"оголошувати"
"легкими в оголошенні", "перевірЕними"
не впевнений що доречно використовувати тут

title: ✨ Add additonal OpenAPI metadata parameters to `FastAPI` class, shown on the automatic API docs UI
body:
As described in #843 that I needed and stumbled upon. This PR adds extra metadata parameters to the code and docs. So now you can specify contact and license information as well as terms of service.

I tried to keep the naming of the parameters in line with the [specification](https://swagger.io/specification/), but unfortunately `license` is a Python built-in, so I went with `license_info` instead.

review_comments: Is there a reason for changing the fields `title`, `version` and `description` here?
Don't we need to validate the `keys` on the `contact` and `license_info`?
Yes, the title and description did not reflect what they should be (`My Super Project`) imo.
We could, but that is not really done for other arguments either.

```suggestion
    terms_of_service: Optional[str] = None,
```

> Do we have the same problem on next exception handler (`RequestValidationError`)?
> 
> But nice catch!

@Kludex No, we don't. Next exception handler is correct:

```Bash
manlix@lab:~$ curl http://localhost:8000/items/incorrect
{"detail":[{"loc":["path","item_id"],"msg":"value is not a valid integer","type":"type_error.integer"}]}
```

Output in logs has expected details in plain text:

```Bash
OMG! The client sent invalid data!: 1 validation error for Request
path -> item_id
  value is not a valid integer (type=type_error.integer)
INFO:     127.0.0.1:50902 - "GET /items/incorrect HTTP/1.1" 422 Unprocessable Entity
```

Awesome, good catch @manlix ! :rocket: :cake: 

Thanks for the help @Kludex ! :coffee:

📝 Docs preview for commit 505c480770f2e59cf5abb76e3c708bc3867221b3 at: https://5f27d491ff928debb1cb91ae--fastapi.netlify.app
A number of these are talking about "the client". Is that not more of an "it"?

Thanks a lot! :mag: :memo: 

I want to have a welcoming space in the FastAPI community and I don't want to have language that might feel unwelcoming. :rocket: 

At the same time, one of my main objectives in the docs is to reduce ambiguity to make it as easy to learn and understand as possible, even for people that might not be native English speakers (like myself), so I updated those examples a bit to make them non-gender specific without having to deal with the ambiguity of possible plurals.

title: add support for injecting HTTPConnection
body:
Hi! 

**TL;DR**:
This PR adds support for injecting `starlette.requests.HTTPConnection` into FastAPI views/dependencies.

**Long reasoning**:
I'm building an application that defines a REST + websocket interface to the outside world. And as storages for this application, I use Redis + PostgreSQL directly throught the drivers (aioredis + asyncpg). Connections are managed through pools that I store in application's state (`app.state.pg_pool`/` app.state.redis_pool`) and queries are made using custom gateways that I initialize in dependencies. Currently FastAPI does not allow using` HTTPConnection` in views, so I need to define duplicate dependencies to access pools via HTTP `Request` and` Websocket`, or use dynamic attributes that break typing/autocompletion, so I don't like this approach. With this change, it is possible to define a single dependency for accessing common request attributes such as app/state/url/headers/etc.

@Enacero great PR! Was looking for something like this in `FastAPI`. Do you think it is possible to include in the factory the `default` parameter to `orjson`?
@victoraugustolls yes, I will add this with next commit for this pull request.
📝 Docs preview for commit c6811866c97cd966402466575481641f06f14fb1 at: https://5f35742a3eeebddf0e5f82c6--fastapi.netlify.app

@victoraugustolls I've tried a lot of variants, but found out, that it's unnecessary to add default to orjson factory, since every response in fast_api is being pre encoded, so that all possible types are already converted by json_encoder and if type is not pre serializable  by json_encoder, error with be raised. So for now, it's useless to add default to orjson factory.

Hi! Why useless? For example, asyncpg returns an instance of "Record" class when you fetch a row from the database. This class is not json encodable but with by passing a default to orjson by casting it to a dict, it is.
@victoraugustolls The thing is that the response is fisrt encoded by json_encoder and than passed to orjson.dumps. So if it's not encoded by json_encoder, it won't be passed to orjson.dumps
@Enacero now I understand! Thanks for your explanation!
review_comments:
why not just extend the `__init__` ?

```python
    def __init__(self, option, **kwds):
        self.option = option
        super().__init__(**kwds)
```
so option is available for ORJSON
@raphaelauv I need somehow to store option so that when ORJson response is being initiated, it must get option from context not from constructor

title: change "methods" assignment in APIRoute class
body:
For better performance, I've removed cascading and replaced it with creating a set manually.
I've tested it for safety and found my idea has better performance ESPECIALLY when the input `methods` is `None`. It creates a set in about half the previously required time for cascading.

And thanks for the comment @ycd ! :mag: 

This optimization would not really affect much, as that code is only called at application startup, once per path operation. It is not called again later per request. So I prefer to keep it simpler, as it is now.

But thanks for the interest! :coffee:
review_comments:
In the original code , `self.methods`'s type is set .
In my opinion, this is done to build a unique set of input parameters.

in this part `{method.upper() for method in methods}` , I changed inputs and used set to create a unique set instead of creating a `list` of changed parameters and cascading it to set.

title: Initial setup for Polish language
body:
Setup for docs translation  to Polish language. 
- I checked if there are no existing Pull request and issues with the translation.
- Followed the documentation on translation contribution.
- Created venv, run the venv, pip installed and run the scripts,
- Checked for language code according to ISO 639-1 codes
- Run the script for new language
- Created Pull Request

review_comments:
```suggestion
Na saída, temos:
```
I can't properly suggest on my phone but there's a typo on "onda" -> "onde".

```suggestion
E colocar em um arquivo `main.py`, você iria chamar o `uvicorn` assim:
```
What do you say?

```suggestion
* Escreva um **decorador de operação de rota** (como `@app.get("/")`).
```

To me, it could be even better with something like:
```suggestion
* Coloque o **decorador que define a operação** (como `@app.get("/")`).
```

I think there's an error with the original doc in English. As far as I'm concerned the correct word should be  "below", not "above" since the full example could be:

```
@app.get("/")
def root(): 
    ...
```
So, the full line would be:
```suggestion
* Escreva uma **função para a operação da rota** (como `def root(): ...`) abaixo.
```
What do you think? Am I missing something?
I really liked the second option, is very clear, thanks!

Thanks for all the work @graingert! :clap: :cake: 

I refactored a couple of things, to keep the same style of import/reexports in the rest of the places (without `__all__`). And I refactored the re-implementation of `asyncio.gather()` to simplify it and inline it a bit as the utility functions are not re-exported either way.

I also pinned Starlette to the minimum version that supports AnyIO (`0.15.0`) to make it easier for others to gradually migrate. I'll make a release with this. And then I'll upgrade Starlette and make another release.

This will be available in a couple of hours in FastAPI `0.69.0`. :tada:

> I also pinned Starlette to the minimum version that supports AnyIO (`0.15.0`) to make it easier for others to gradually migrate.

You should definitely pin to 0.16.0, the breaking change is around the TestClient(async_backend) kwarg which didn't exist before 0.15.0
review_comments: Would need to flagged as a breaking change in the change log? (message for tiangolo he makes the change logs)
It's an `starlette` requirement, so I guess we don't need it here?
starlette walked back on the removal of this function
this depends on `anyio[trio]` now, which isn't a starlette requirement

```suggestion
    "starlette ==0.16.0",
```

```suggestion
    "anyio[trio] >=3.2.1,<4",
```

title: ✨ Enable Pydantic's serialization mode for responses, add support for Pydantic's `computed_field`, better OpenAPI for response models, proper required attributes, better generated clients
body:
✨ Enable Pydantic's serialization mode for responses, add support for Pydantic's `computed_field`.

Ref: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/usage/computed_fields/
comments:
@tiangolo This change seems to break parts of OpenAPI / JSONSchema generation.

Specifically, fields that should not be "required" in the schema now are.

Simple repro:

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI()


class Item(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    is_offer: None | bool = None


@app.get("/item", response_model=Item)
async def get_item():
    return {
        "name": "Foo",
        "price": 42.0,
        "is_offer": True,
    }
```

In the above, the generated schema for the `Item` model has `is_offer` listed in the `required` field of the JSON schema, even though the [docs for Pydantic 2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/migration/#required-optional-and-nullable-fields) indicate that this should *not* be required, and it was working as expected in FastAPI 0.100.x

Can make a separate issue for this, but thought I'd post here first.

title: ✨ Add support for disabling the separation of input and output JSON Schemas in OpenAPI with Pydantic v2
